concept:
Tested 4 ? Patient presents with sudden onset of severe, diffuse abdominal pain. Exam reveals peritoneal signs and abdominal (AXR) reveals free air under the diaphram. Management?
Diagnosis: Perforated viscus, likely stomach
Treatment: Emergent laparotomy to repair
Most likely cause of acute lower GI bleed in patients greater than 40 years-old: Diverticulosis
Can Diverticulosis cause GI bleeding? No Yes
What is the diagnostic modality used when ultrasound (U/S) is equivocal for cholecystitis? HIDA scan
Sentinal loop in AXR indicates: Acute pancreatitis
Risk factors for cholethiasis are:
Fat
Female
Fertile
Forty
Flatulent
Inspiratory arrest during palpation of the upper right quadrant (RUQ) indicates: Murphy's sign, seen in acute cholecystitis
Organism that is the most common cause of diarrhea: Campylobacter
Recent aintibiotic use causing diarrhea: Clostridium difficile
Organism causing diarrhea picked up from camping: Giardia
Traveler's diarrhea comes from what organism: ETEC
Organism causing diarrhea picked up from church picnics or mayonaise: Staphylococcus aureus
Organism causing diarrhea picked up eating uncooked hamburgers: Eschericia Coli (O157:H7)
Organism causing diarrhea picked up eating fried rice: Bacillus Cereus
Organism causing diarrhea picked up eating poultry/eggs: Salmonella
Organism causing diarrhea picked up eating raw seafood:
Vibrio
HAV
Organism causing diarrhea for persons with AIDS:
Isospora
Cryptosporidium
Mycobacterium avium complex (MAC)
Organism causing diarrhea with pseudoappendicitis: Yersinia
Patient is:
24 year-old
Jewish
male
Patient complains of:
pain
watery diarrhea after meals
Patient's exam shows:
fistulas between the bowel and skin
nodular lesions on his tibias
indicating: Crohn's disease
Inflammatory disease of the colon with increased risk of colon cancer is: Ulcerative colitis
Extraintestinal manifestations of IBD are:
Uveitis
Ankylosing spondylitis (AS)
Pyodema gangrenosum
Erythema nodosum
Primary sclerosing cholangitis
Medical treatment for IBD is: 5-aminosalicylic acid +/- sulfasalazine and steroids during acute exacerbations
Difference between Mallory-Weiss and Boerhaave tears is:
Mallory-Weiss: Superficial tear in the esophageal mucosa
Boerhaave: Full-thickness esophageal rupture
Charcot's triad is:
Right upper quadrant (RUQ) pain
Jaundice
Fever/chills in the setting of ascending cholangitis
Reynold's pentad is:
Charcot's triad
Right upper quadrant (RUQ) pain
Jaundice
Fever/chills in the setting of ascending cholangitis
Shock
Mental status changes
With suppurative ascending cholangitis
Medical treatment for hepatic encephalopathy is:
Decreased protein intake
Lactulos
Neomycin
First step in the management of a patient with an acute GI bleed is: Establish the ABCs
A patient
had an illness with bloody diarrhea
is 4 years old
has oliguria
shows petechia
shows jaundice Most likely diagnosis and cause? Hemolytic uremic syndrome due to E. Coli O157:H7
Post-HBV exposure treatment is: HBV immunoglobulin
Classic causes of drug-induced hepatitis are:
TB medications
INH
Rifampin
pyrazinamide
Acetaminophen
Tetracycline
A 40 year-old obese female presents with elevated alkaline phosphatase, elevated bilirubin, pruritus, dark urine, and clay-colored stools indicating: Biliary tract obstruction
Which is the hernia with highest risk of incarceration?
Indirect
Direct
Femoral Femoral hernia
A 50 year-old man with a history of alchohol abuse presents with boring epigastric pain that radiates to the back and is relieved by sitting forward. Diagnosis? Treatment?
Suspicion:Acute pancreatis
Confirm with: Elevated amylase and lipase
Treatment:
Make patient NPS and give IV fluids
Oxygen
Analgesia
"Tincture of time"
